name: Drawer
goal: Edge-attached panel that slides in from top, right, bottom, or left for filter panels, settings, inspectors, carts, and navigation.
description: A sliding panel anchored to one of the four viewport edges. In modal mode wraps a native <dialog> with showModal() for automatic focus trap, scrim, and Escape handling. In non-modal mode uses a positioned div. Supports four sides, four sizes, an optional scrim dismiss guard, trigger slot, header/body/footer slots, and programmatic show/hide/toggle methods.

props.name: open
props.description: When true the drawer is visible; setting to false closes it.

props.name: side
props.description: Which viewport edge the drawer attaches to. start = inline-start (left in LTR), end = inline-end (right in LTR), top, bottom.

props.name: modal
props.description: When true (default) uses <dialog showModal()> for focus trap, scrim, and native Escape. When false uses a positioned div.

props.name: size
props.description: Controls the drawer's cross-axis dimension. sm=24rem, md=32rem, lg=48rem, full=100% of the relevant viewport dimension.

props.name: dismissible
props.description: When true (default), Escape and scrim/backdrop click close the drawer. When false, only programmatic hide() works.

events.name: ce-drawer-open
events.description: Fires after the drawer becomes visible.

methods.name: show
methods.description: Opens the drawer (equivalent to setting open=true).

methods.name: hide
methods.description: Closes the drawer (equivalent to removing the open attribute).

slots.name: trigger
slots.description: Element that opens the drawer on click. Receives aria-haspopup and aria-expanded.

slots.name: header
slots.description: Header area: title, logo, branding. Used as the dialog's accessible label via aria-labelledby.
Drawer body content. Scrollable if content overflows.

slots.name: footer
slots.description: Action area: buttons, status. Hidden when the slot is empty.

cssVariables.name: --ce-transition
cssVariables.description: Transition timing used for the close button hover state.

cssVariables.name: --ce-space-5
cssVariables.description: Header, body, and footer horizontal padding.

cssVariables.name: --ce-drawer-easing
cssVariables.description: Slide animation easing function. Default ease.

cssVariables.name: --ce-drawer-scrim
cssVariables.description: Backdrop scrim color in modal mode. Default rgba(0,0,0,0.45).

cssVariables.name: --ce-drawer-z
cssVariables.description: z-index for non-modal positioned panel. Default 400.

globalDependencies.purpose: Opens the dialog in the top-layer with a built-in focus trap and backdrop.
Removes the dialog from the top-layer.
Caches the previously-focused element so focus can be restored on close.

sideEffects.description: Calls dialog.showModal() and dialog.close() to manage the native top-layer in modal mode.
sideEffects.reason: Required to leverage the browser's built-in top-layer, focus-trap, backdrop, and Escape handling.

sideEffects.description: Returns focus to document.activeElement captured at open time.
sideEffects.reason: WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.3: focus must return to the trigger element after a dialog closes.

sideEffects.description: In non-modal mode, attaches a document keydown listener to handle Escape when dismissible=true.
sideEffects.reason: Non-modal panels are not in the top-layer so the browser does not fire cancel for them.

a11y.notes: Modal mode: native <dialog showModal()> supplies role=dialog, focus trap, and Escape handling automatically. aria-modal=true and aria-labelledby pointing to the header slot wrapper are added to the dialog element. Non-modal mode: the panel div carries role=dialog, aria-modal=false, and the same aria-labelledby. The close button has aria-label='Close'. The slotted trigger receives aria-haspopup=dialog and aria-expanded. Focus returns to the trigger element on close.
